title: sambucus williamsii var. coreana
intro: Sambucus williamsii var. coreana, also known as Korean elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, specifically in Korea and China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, thanks to its high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and anthocyanins. In traditional herbal preparations, the berries, flowers, and leaves of Sambucus williamsii var. coreana are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
